judgment.summary: Amazon demonstrates robust growth in revenue and earnings, supported by healthy margins and a strong analyst consensus, despite a significant debt load.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 68.2%; next earnings date is 2026-07-30.
positives: Earnings growth 74.8% YoY and quarterly earnings growth 76.7% indicate strong profitability expansion.
Profit margin 12.2% and operating margin 13.1% show efficient operations.
Return on equity 24.3% signifies effective use of shareholder investments.
Forward P/E of 27.14 is attractive given the high growth rates.
Consensus rating is strong buy from 62 analysts with a mean target of $312.63.
negatives: Debt/Equity ratio of 53.30 indicates substantial leverage.
Total debt $235.54B exceeds total cash $143.09B, resulting in negative net debt.
PEG ratio of 1.83 suggests the stock may be somewhat richly valued relative to its expected earnings growth.
Current ratio of 1.18 and quick ratio of 0.97 are only marginally above 1, indicating tight liquidity.

news.paragraph: Amazon's stock is garnering positive attention on Wall Street, with recent headlines highlighting its expansion into AI-powered shopping guides and a strong performance relative to peers like Meta. The company's strategic moves in artificial intelligence appear to be resonating, contributing to a bullish sentiment among analysts. Investors will be watching to see if this momentum continues, especially with the next earnings date approaching.

headlines.title: As Meta Misfires And Nvidia Shines, Amazon Stock Has Wall Street Smiling. Here's Why.
Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) Expands with AI-Powered Shopping Guides
